summary: Response times for bedbug, mice, and roach complaints worsened through FY23 but then improved dramatically as NYCHA consolidated its exterminators into one Pest Control Department, focused on identifying root causes of infestations, and — per NYCHA's FY26 report — added daily supervisor check-in meetings and cross-training programs. This buildout was driven by pest-response deadlines in NYCHA's 2019 consent agreement with the federal Department of Housing and Urban Development.

sources.label: NYCHA, Preliminary Fiscal 2026 Mayor's Management Report

oneLiner: increasing after NYCHA consolidated pest control under its 2019 HUD agreement
